content.problemStatement:
Chef is stuck in the wavey world of polynomials. You are given all NNN roots of a polynomial P(x)=∏i=1N(x−ai)P(x) = \prod_{i=1}^N (x - a_i)P(x)=∏i=1N​(x−ai​). The roots are pairwise distinct integers, but they are not given in any particular order.

To help Chef escape, you should answer QQQ queries (numbered 111 through QQQ). For each valid iii, in the iii-th query, you are given an integer xix_ixi​ and you have to determine whether P(xi)P(x_i)P(xi​) is positive, negative or 000.
constraints: 1 <= N, Q <= 2*10^5 (sum of N and Q over all test cases fits in memory limits)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains two integers N and Q. The second line contains N distinct integers a1, a2, …, aN – the roots of the polynomial. Each of the next Q lines contains a single integer xi, the point at which the polynomial must be evaluated.
content.outputFormat: For each query, output a line containing one of the strings: "POSITIVE" if P(xi) > 0, "NEGATIVE" if P(xi) < 0, or "0" if P(xi) = 0.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The polynomial is (x-1)(x-3)(x-5)(x-100). Evaluating at each query gives the listed signs.
First test case: P(x)=x. Values at -1,0,2 are -1,0,2 → NEGATIVE,0,POSITIVE. Second test case: P(x)=(x+1)(x-2). At 0 the product is -2 (NEGATIVE), at 2 it is 0.
P(x)=(x+5)x(x-5). Signs follow from the number of negative factors.

solution.approach: The sign of P(x)=∏(x‑ai) depends only on how many factors are negative because all factors are non‑zero unless x equals a root. After sorting the roots, for a query x we locate the first root ≥ x using binary search. If that root equals x, the product is zero. Otherwise the number of roots strictly greater than x is N‑lower_bound_index. If this count is even the product is positive, otherwise negative. Sorting takes O(N log N) per test case and each query is answered in O(log N).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N + Q log N) per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the sorted roots.
